outlets.name: CNN
outlets.headline: Trump considers Kharg Island seizure as he claims Iran agreed to 'most of' US demands
outlets.summary: CNN's Day 30 PM liveblog covers Trump's bombshell Kharg Island threat aboard Air Force One, IDF evening Tehran wave causing power blackouts, IAEA confirming Khondab reactor destroyed, and UNIFIL peacekeeper killed in Lebanon.

outlets.name: Washington Post
outlets.headline: Pentagon prepares for weeks of ground operations in Iran
outlets.summary: WaPo broke the story that the Pentagon is war-gaming ground raids on Kharg Island and Larak Island, with the 82nd Airborne as a potential force. White House: 'maximum optionality' for president.

outlets.name: NPR
outlets.headline: Pope Leo XIV rejects claims God justifies war in powerful Palm Sunday homily
outlets.summary: NPR covers Pope Leo's historic Palm Sunday homily directly challenging religious justifications for war, widely interpreted as a rebuke of Hegseth's Pentagon prayer service. Islamabad summit and Iran NPT withdrawal threat also highlighted.

outlets.name: New York Times
outlets.headline: As Iran War Enters Second Month, World Faces Potential Wider Conflagration
outlets.summary: NYT analysis examines how the conflict has exceeded early predictions, with the Hormuz blockade, Houthi entry, and Gulf industrial strikes raising fears of a broader regional war.

outlets.name: Fox News
outlets.headline: Trump considers sending up to 10,000 more troops to Middle East during Iran war
outlets.summary: Fox News live coverage emphasizes Trump's strength narrative and the potential for a decisive ground blow. Reports Pentagon prepping for Kharg Island raid as a war-winning move.

outlets.name: NY Post
outlets.headline: IDF days away from knocking out all 'critical' targets in Iran
outlets.summary: NY Post focuses on IDF Spokesperson Defrin's statement that Iran can no longer manufacture new ballistic missiles, framing the campaign as a military success approaching its conclusion.

outlets.name: Washington Times
outlets.headline: Ghalibaf threatens to 'punish' any US soldier who enters Iran as Pentagon preps ground options
outlets.summary: Conservative outlet reports Iranian Parliament Speaker's threat in context of Pentagon's ground operations planning, framing Iran's warning as bluster against US military superiority.

outlets.name: Breitbart
outlets.headline: Trump's Iran Campaign Approaches Climax — IDF: Missile Factories Destroyed
outlets.summary: Breitbart emphasizes the military destruction of Iranian missile production and frames it as a major Trump policy success.

outlets.name: Al Jazeera
outlets.headline: US-Israel war on Iran: What's happening on day 30 of attacks?
outlets.summary: Al Jazeera's comprehensive Day 30 summary covers the Beersheba chemical plant scare, Bandar Khamir port strike, Houthi attacks, and diplomatic paralysis as Pakistan, Turkey, Egypt and Saudi Arabia seek de-escalation.

outlets.name: BBC
outlets.headline: Iran war: Missile hits Israeli chemical plant as conflict enters second month
outlets.summary: BBC leads with the ADAMA chemical plant incident and Beersheba missile strike, framing the conflict's entry into its second month as a significant milestone amid growing humanitarian concerns.

outlets.name: Reuters
outlets.headline: Iran fires missile at Beersheba, hitting chemical plant; IRGC strikes Gulf aluminum facilities
outlets.summary: Reuters covers both the Beersheba strike and the IRGC aluminium plant attacks in Bahrain and UAE, while noting Iran's FM denied direct talks with the US despite intermediary messages.

outlets.name: AP
outlets.headline: Iran war at one month: 1,200+ dead in Lebanon, 1,900+ in Iran as conflict enters second month
outlets.summary: AP provides a comprehensive death toll summary at the one-month mark, noting the conflict has now killed over 3,000 people across Iran, Lebanon, Israel, Gulf states and Iraq.

outlets.name: Times of Israel
outlets.headline: Iran missile attacks on south lightly hurt 11 in Beersheba, cause blaze in nearby chemical plant
outlets.summary: ToI Day 30 liveblog leads with the Beersheba-Ne'ot Hovav missile incident, IDF killing two Hezbollah communications leaders in Beirut, and IDF Spokesperson Defrin's claim that Iran's missile production capability is destroyed.

outlets.name: Haaretz
outlets.headline: Israeli Support for Iran War Wanes After Early Enthusiasm, Poll Shows
outlets.summary: Haaretz poll shows Israeli public support for the Iran war has declined significantly after initial enthusiasm, particularly among center-left voters. 63% still support continuing but opposition has doubled since Day 1.

outlets.name: Ynet
outlets.headline: Iran targets southern Israel: impacts in Be'er Sheva, industrial plant hit in Neot Hovav
outlets.summary: Ynet covers the Beersheba missile and Ne'ot Hovav chemical plant fire, noting the emergency response and all-clear from Environment Minister Silman. 11 hospitalized from the Beersheba strike.

outlets.name: Press TV
outlets.headline: Iran weighing withdrawal from Nuclear Non-Proliferation Treaty after US-Israeli strikes on nuclear sites
outlets.summary: Iranian state broadcaster covers parliament's push for NPT withdrawal, framing it as a defensive response to US-Israeli nuclear site strikes. Tehran MPs advance legislation to leave the NPT framework and join SCO/BRICS nuclear cooperation instead.

outlets.name: IRNA
outlets.headline: IRGC issues ultimatum: US must condemn university strikes by noon Monday or face escalation
outlets.summary: Iranian state news agency covers the IRGC's formal ultimatum threatening Texas A&M Qatar, Northwestern Qatar, and NYU Abu Dhabi as 'legitimate targets.' Iran's position: US-affiliated universities are extensions of the military-industrial complex.

outlets.name: Tasnim News
outlets.headline: Iran begins considering withdrawal from NPT amid ongoing US-Israeli strikes on nuclear facilities
outlets.summary: Tasnim covers the parliamentary NPT withdrawal debate, quoting MP Rezaei: 'The NPT has offered us no advantage whatsoever.' Iranian officials stress this is about IAEA inspection rights, not weapons development.
